# Setup Production Service: {{SERVICE_NAME}}

**Generated by**: Architect Khang (lead), SRE Minh (infrastructure), Mary Analyst (risk)
**Date**: 2026-03-19
**Service**: {{SERVICE_NAME}} (Authentication Microservice)
**Phase**: 2-build

---

## Step 01: Architecture Design

### Service Purpose

{{SERVICE_NAME}} handles authentication and authorization for the platform:
- User login/logout (email + OAuth2)
- JWT token issuance and validation
- Session management with Redis
- Role-based access control (RBAC)
- API key management for service-to-service auth

### Architecture Pattern

**Chosen**: Clean Architecture with Repository Pattern

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    API Layer (FastAPI)                  │
│  POST /auth/login  POST /auth/logout  GET /auth/verify  │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────────┐
│                  Application Layer                       │
│    AuthService  TokenService  SessionService  RBACService│
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────────┐
│                    Domain Layer                          │
│      User  Session  Role  Permission  APIKey            │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────────┐
│                Infrastructure Layer                      │
│   PostgreSQL   Redis   JWT Library   bcrypt   LDAP      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Technology Selection Rationale

| Component | Choice | Reason |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| Language | Python 3.11 | Team expertise, rich auth libraries |
| Framework | FastAPI | Async, OpenAPI auto-gen, type safety |
| Database | PostgreSQL 15 | ACID, Row-Level Security for RBAC |
| Cache | Redis 7 | Session store, token blacklist |
| Auth | JWT + Refresh tokens | Stateless auth with refresh rotation |
| Hashing | bcrypt (cost=12) | OWASP recommended |
| Migration | Alembic | Version-controlled schema |

### SLO Targets

- Availability: 99.9% (43 min downtime/month)
- Latency p95: < 100ms for token verification
- Latency p95: < 500ms for login
- Error rate: < 0.1%

---

## Step 02: API Design & Database

### API Endpoints

```
POST   /auth/register          — Register new user
POST   /auth/login             — Email/password login
POST   /auth/logout            — Invalidate session
POST   /auth/refresh           — Rotate refresh token
GET    /auth/verify            — Verify JWT token
GET    /auth/me                — Get current user profile
POST   /auth/password/reset    — Initiate password reset
POST   /auth/password/confirm  — Complete password reset

GET    /auth/api-keys          — List API keys
POST   /auth/api-keys          — Create API key
DELETE /auth/api-keys/{id}     — Revoke API key

GET    /auth/roles              — List roles (admin)
POST   /auth/roles              — Create role (admin)
PUT    /auth/roles/{id}        — Update role (admin)
```

### Database Schema

```sql
-- Users table
CREATE TABLE users (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    full_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    is_verified BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(),
    last_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);

-- Sessions table
CREATE TABLE sessions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    refresh_token_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    user_agent VARCHAR(512),
    ip_address INET,
    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);

-- Roles table
CREATE TABLE roles (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);

-- Role-User mapping
CREATE TABLE user_roles (
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    role_id UUID REFERENCES roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, role_id)
);

-- Permissions table
CREATE TABLE permissions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    resource VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    action VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    UNIQUE(resource, action)
);

-- Role-Permission mapping
CREATE TABLE role_permissions (
    role_id UUID REFERENCES roles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    permission_id UUID REFERENCES permissions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    PRIMARY KEY (role_id, permission_id)
);

-- API Keys table
CREATE TABLE api_keys (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    key_hash VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    scopes JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
    last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);
```

### Indexes

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_created_at ON users(created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_user_id ON sessions(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_expires_at ON sessions(expires_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_api_keys_user_id ON api_keys(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_api_keys_key_hash ON api_keys(key_hash);
```

---

## Step 03: Build & Deployment

### Docker Configuration

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim AS base
WORKDIR /app
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    gcc libpq-dev && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

FROM base AS builder
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --user --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

FROM base AS runtime
COPY --from=builder /root/.local /root/.local
COPY . .
ENV PATH=/root/.local/bin:$PATH
RUN useradd --create-home appuser && chown -R appuser:appuser /app
USER appuser
EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["uvicorn", "api.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
```

### CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions)

```yaml
name: CI/CD
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.11' }
      - run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - run: pytest tests/ -v --cov=. --cov-report=xml
      - uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3

  build:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with: role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::123456:role/github-actions
      - run: |
          docker build -t $ECR_REGISTRY/{{SERVICE_NAME}}:$GITHUB_SHA .
          docker push $ECR_REGISTRY/{{SERVICE_NAME}}:$GITHUB_SHA

  deploy-staging:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    steps:
      - run: |
          kubectl set image deployment/{{SERVICE_NAME}} \
            {{SERVICE_NAME}}=$ECR_REGISTRY/{{SERVICE_NAME}}:$GITHUB_SHA \
            -n staging
```

### Helm Chart (EKS)

```yaml
# values.yaml (staging)
image:
  repository: 123456.dkr.ecr.ap-southeast-1.amazonaws.com/{{SERVICE_NAME}}
  tag: latest
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

replicaCount: 2

resources:
  limits: { cpu: 500m, memory: 512Mi }
  requests: { cpu: 200m, memory: 256Mi }

autoscaling:
  enabled: true
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 10
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 70

env:
  DATABASE_URL: secretRef:postgres-credentials:url
  REDIS_URL: secretRef:redis-credentials:url
  JWT_SECRET: secretRef:jwt-secret:key
  JWT_EXPIRY: "15m"
  REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRY: "7d"
```

---

## Step 04: Reliability & Observability

### Resilience Patterns

**1. Retry with exponential backoff** (token validation):
```python
@retry(stop=stop_after_attempt(3), wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, max=10))
async def verify_token(self, token: str) -> Optional[User]:
    ...
```

**2. Circuit breaker** (external OAuth provider):
```python
breaker = CircuitBreaker(
    failure_threshold=5,
    recovery_timeout=30,
    expected_exception=OAuthProviderError
)
```

**3. Bulkhead** (separate thread pools for auth vs token validation):
```python
auth_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=50)
token_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=200)
```

### Observability Stack

**Metrics** (Prometheus):
- `auth_login_total{status="success|failure", method="email|oauth"}`
- `auth_token_verification_duration_seconds{quantile="0.5|0.95|0.99"}`
- `auth_active_sessions_total`
- `auth_api_key_usage_total`

**Tracing** (OpenTelemetry + Jaeger):
- Span for each API endpoint
- Distributed trace across auth → Redis → PostgreSQL
- Correlation ID propagation

**Logging** (structured JSON):
```json
{
  "level": "INFO",
  "service": "{{SERVICE_NAME}}",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-19T15:30:00Z",
  "event": "login_success",
  "user_id": "uuid-here",
  "method": "email",
  "duration_ms": 145,
  "correlation_id": "trace-uuid"
}
```

**Health Checks**:
- `/health/live` — Liveness probe (process alive)
- `/health/ready` — Readiness probe (DB + Redis connected)

---

## Step 05: Security Configuration

### Authentication Flow

```
1. User submits email + password
2. Server: verify email format → check rate limit → bcrypt verify
3. If valid: generate JWT (15min) + refresh token (7 days)
4. Store refresh token hash in sessions table
5. Return { access_token, refresh_token, expires_in }
```

### RBAC Permissions

| Role | Permissions |
|------|-------------|
| user | read:profile, update:profile |
| moderator | + read:users, update:users |
| admin | + create:users, delete:users, manage:roles |
| service | + read:all, api:internal |

### Kubernetes Network Policies

```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: {{SERVICE_NAME}}-network-policy
  namespace: production
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: {{SERVICE_NAME}}
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
    - Egress
  ingress:
    - from:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              name: api-gateway
      ports:
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 8000
  egress:
    - to:
        - podSelector:
            matchLabels:
              app: postgresql
        - podSelector:
            matchLabels:
              app: redis
```

### Secrets Management (AWS Secrets Manager)

```yaml
# External Secret Operator
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  name: {{SERVICE_NAME}}-secrets
spec:
  refreshInterval: 1h
  secretStoreRef:
    name: aws-secrets-manager
    kind: ClusterSecretStore
  target:
    name: postgres-credentials
  data:
    - secretKey: url
      remoteRef:
        key: production/{{SERVICE_NAME}}/database
        property: connection_string
```

**1 RED item**: Load testing not yet performed (scheduled for staging deployment)

### Runbook Summary

**Service**: {{SERVICE_NAME}} | **On-Call**: SRE Team
**Runbook URL**: `runbooks/{{SERVICE_NAME}}/index.md`

| Scenario | Procedure |
|----------|-----------|
| Service down | Check pod status → check DB/Redis connectivity → escalate |
| High latency | Check Redis ping → check DB query times → scale horizontally |
| Token validation failures | Check Redis cluster health → check JWT secret rotation |
| Brute force detection | Check failed login rate → block IP via WAF |
| Token refresh storm | Check for token expiry stampede → implement sliding window |

### GO / NO-GO Decision

| Criterion | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| All functional tests pass | PASS | 47/47 tests passed |
| Security review completed | PASS | No critical findings |
| SRE sign-off | PASS | Minh approved |
| Load test scheduled | CONDITIONAL | Will complete within 1 week of deploy |
| Runbook reviewed | PASS | 5 scenarios documented |

**DECISION: GO (conditional)** — Service may deploy to production after staging soak test (48 hours, no issues). Load test to be completed within 1 week.
